translations.text: And Barnabas went forth to Tarsus, to seek for Saul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having found him, he brought him to Antioch, and it came to pass that they a whole year did assemble together in the assembly, and taught a great multitude, the disciples also were divinely called first in Antioch Christians.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in those days there came from Jerusalem prophets to Antioch,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one of them, by name Agabus, having stood up, did signify through the Spirit a great dearth is about to be throughout all the world -- which also came to pass in the time of Claudius Caesar --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the disciples, according as any one was prospering, determined each of them to send for ministration to the brethren dwelling in Judea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which also they did, having sent unto the elders by the hand of Barnabas and Saul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And about that time, Herod the king put forth his hands, to do evil to certain of those of the assembly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he killed James, the brother of John, with the sword,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen that it is pleasing to the Jews, he added to lay hold of Peter also -- and they were the days of the unleavened food --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom also having seized, he did put in prison, having delivered [him] to four quaternions of soldiers to guard him, intending after the passover to bring him forth to the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Peter, therefore, indeed, was kept in the prison, and fervent prayer was being made by the assembly unto God for him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when Herod was about to bring him forth, the same night was Peter sleeping between two soldiers, having been bound with two chains, guards also before the door were keeping the prison,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a messenger of the Lord stood by, and a light shone in the buildings, and having smitten Peter on the side, he raised him up, saying, 'Rise in haste,' and his chains fell from off [his] hands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The messenger also said to him, 'Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals;' and he did so; and he saith to him, 'Put thy garment round and be following me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gone forth, he was following him, and he knew not that it is true that which is done through the messenger, and was thinking he saw a vision,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having passed through a first ward, and a second, they came unto the iron gate that is leading to the city, which of its own accord did open to them, and having gone forth, they went on through one street, and immediately the messenger departed from him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter having come to himself, said, 'Now I have known of a truth that the Lord did sent forth His messenger, and did deliver me out of the hand of Herod, and all the expectation of the people of the Jews;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: also, having considered, he came unto the house of Mary, the mother of John, who is surnamed Mark, where there were many thronged together and praying.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter having knocked at the door of the porch, there came a damsel to hearken, by name Rhoda,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having known the voice of Peter, from the joy she did not open the porch, but having run in, told of the standing of Peter before the porch,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said unto her, 'Thou art mad;' and she was confidently affirming [it] to be so, and they said, 'It is his messenger;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Peter was continuing knocking, and having opened, they saw him, and were astonished,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having beckoned to them with the hand to be silent, he declared to them how the Lord brought him out of the prison, and he said, 'Declare to James and to the brethren these things;' and having gone forth, he went on to another place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And day having come, there was not a little stir among the soldiers what then was become of Peter,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Herod having sought for him, and not having found, having examined the guards, did command [them] to be led away to punishment, and having gone down from Judea to Caesarea, he was abiding [there].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Herod was highly displeased with the Tyrians and Sidonians, and with one accord they came unto him, and having made a friend of Blastus, who [is] over the bed-chambers of the king, they were asking peace, because of their country being nourished from the king's;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on a set day, Herod having arrayed himself in kingly apparel, and having sat down upon the tribunal, was making an oration unto them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the populace were shouting, 'The voice of a god, and not of a man;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and presently there smote him a messenger of the Lord, because he did not give the glory to God, and having been eaten of worms, he expired.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the word of God did grow and did multiply,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Barnabas and Saul did turn back out of Jerusalem, having fulfilled the ministration, having taken also with [them] John, who was surnamed Mark.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were certain in Antioch, in the assembly there, prophets and teachers; both Barnabas, and Simeon who is called Niger, and Lucius the Cyrenian, Manaen also -- Herod the tetrarch's foster-brother -- and Saul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in their ministering to the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, 'Separate ye to me both Barnabas and Saul to the work to which I have called them,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then having fasted, and having prayed, and having laid the hands on them, they sent [them] away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These, indeed, then, having been sent forth by the Holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia, thence also they sailed to Cyprus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come unto Salamis, they declared the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews, and they had also John [as] a ministrant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gone through the island unto Paphos, they found a certain magian, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name [is] Bar-Jesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who was with the proconsul Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man; this one having called for Barnabas and Saul, did desire to hear the word of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there withstood them Elymas the magian -- for so is his name interpreted -- seeking to pervert the proconsul from the faith.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Saul -- who also [is] Paul -- having been filled with the Holy Spirit, and having looked stedfastly on him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: said, 'O full of all guile, and all profligacy, son of a devil, enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease perverting the right ways of the Lord?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, lo, a hand of the Lord [is] upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season;' and presently there fell upon him a mist and darkness, and he, going about, was seeking some to lead [him] by the hand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then the proconsul having seen what hath come to pass, did believe, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those about Paul having set sail from Paphos, came to Perga of Pamphylia, and John having departed from them, did turn back to Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having gone through from Perga, came to Antioch of Pisidia, and having gone into the synagogue on the sabbath-day, they sat down,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and after the reading of the law and of the prophets, the chief men of the synagogue sent unto them, saying, 'Men, brethren, if there be a word in you of exhortation unto the people -- say on.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul having risen, and having beckoned with the hand, said, 'Men, Israelites, and those fearing God, hearken:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the God of this people Israel did choose our fathers, and the people He did exalt in their sojourning in the land of Egypt, and with an high arm did He bring them out of it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and about a period of forty years He did suffer their manners in the wilderness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, He did divide by lot to them their land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And after these things, about four hundred and fifty years, He gave judges -- till Samuel the prophet;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thereafter they asked for a king, and God did give to them Saul, son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having removed him, He did raise up to them David for king, to whom also having testified, he said, I found David, the [son] of Jesse, a man according to My heart, who shall do all My will.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Of this one's seed God, according to promise, did raise to Israel a Saviour -- Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: John having first preached, before his coming, a baptism of reformation to all the people of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as John was fulfilling the course, he said, Whom me do ye suppose to be? I am not [he], but, lo, he doth come after me, of whom I am not worthy to loose the sandal of [his] feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Men, brethren, sons of the race of Abraham, and those among you fearing God, to you was the word of this salvation sent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for those dwelling in Jerusalem, and their chiefs, this one not having known, also the voices of the prophets, which every sabbath are being read -- having judged [him] -- did fulfil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and no cause of death having found, they did ask of Pilate that he should be slain,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when they did complete all the things written about him, having taken [him] down from the tree, they laid him in a tomb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God did raise him out of the dead,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was seen for many days of those who did come up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And we to you do proclaim good news -- that the promise made unto the fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God hath in full completed this to us their children, having raised up Jesus, as also in the second Psalm it hath been written, My Son thou art -- I to-day have begotten thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that He did raise him up out of the dead, no more to return to corruption, he hath said thus -- I will give to you the faithful kindnesses of David;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore also in another [place] he saith, Thou shalt not give Thy kind One to see corruption,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for David, indeed, his own generation having served by the will of God, did fall asleep, and was added unto his fathers, and saw corruption,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but he whom God did raise up, did not see corruption.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Let it therefore be known to you, men, brethren, that through this one to you is the forgiveness of sins declared,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and from all things from which ye were not able in the law of Moses to be declared righteous, in this one every one who is believing is declared righteous;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: see, therefore, it may not come upon you that hath been spoken in the prophets:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: See, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish, because a work I -- I do work in your days, a work in which ye may not believe, though any one may declare [it] to you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gone forth out of the synagogue of the Jews, the nations were calling upon [them] that on the next sabbath these sayings may be spoken to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the synagogue having been dismissed, many of the Jews and of the devout proselytes did follow Paul and Barnabas, who, speaking to them, were persuading them to remain in the grace of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on the coming sabbath, almost all the city was gathered together to hear the word of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Jews having seen the multitudes, were filled with zeal, and did contradict the things spoken by Paul -- contradicting and speaking evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And speaking boldly, Paul and Barnabas said, 'To you it was necessary that first the word of God be spoken, and seeing ye do thrust it away, and do not judge yourselves worthy of the life age-during, lo, we do turn to the nations;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for so hath the Lord commanded us: I have set thee for a light of nations -- for thy being for salvation unto the end of the earth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the nations hearing were glad, and were glorifying the word of the Lord, and did believe -- as many as were appointed to life age-during;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the word of the Lord was spread abroad through all the region.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Jews stirred up the devout and honourable women, and the first men of the city, and did raise persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and did put them out from their borders;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having shaken off the dust of their feet against them, came to Iconium,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the disciples were filled with joy and the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass in Iconium, that they did enter together into the synagogue of the Jews, and spake, so that there believed both of Jews and Greeks a great multitude;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the unbelieving Jews did stir up and made evil the souls of the nations against the brethren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: long time, indeed, therefore, did they abide speaking boldly in the Lord, who is testifying to the word of His grace, and granting signs and wonders to come to pass through their hands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the multitude of the city was divided, and some were with the Jews, and some with the apostles,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when there was a purpose both of the nations and of the Jews with their rulers to use [them] despitefully, and to stone them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they having become aware, did flee to the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra, and Derbe, and to the region round about,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there they were proclaiming good news.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a certain man in Lystra, impotent in the feet, was sitting, being lame from the womb of his mother -- who never had walked,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this one was hearing Paul speaking, who, having stedfastly beheld him, and having seen that he hath faith to be saved,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: said with a loud voice, 'Stand up on thy feet upright;' and he was springing and walking,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the multitudes having seen what Paul did, did lift up their voice, in the speech of Lycaonia, saying, 'The gods, having become like men, did come down unto us;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they were calling also Barnabas Zeus, and Paul Hermes, since he was the leader in speaking.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the priest of the Zeus that is before their city, oxen and garlands unto the porches having brought, with the multitudes did wish to sacrifice,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having heard, the apostles Barnabas and Paul, having rent their garments, did spring into the multitude, crying

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saying, 'Men, why these things do ye? and we are men like-affected with you, proclaiming good news to you, from these vanities to turn unto the living God, who made the heaven, and the earth, and the sea, and all the things in them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who in the past generations did suffer all the nations to go on in their ways,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: though, indeed, without witness He did not leave himself, doing good -- from heaven giving rains to us, and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these things saying, scarcely did they restrain the multitudes from sacrificing to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there came thither, from Antioch and Iconium, Jews, and they having persuaded the multitudes, and having stoned Paul, drew him outside of the city, having supposed him to be dead;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the disciples having surrounded him, having risen he entered into the city, and on the morrow he went forth with Barnabas to Derbe.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Having proclaimed good news also to that city, and having discipled many, they turned back to Lystra, and Iconium, and Antioch,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: confirming the souls of the disciples, exhorting to remain in the faith, and that through many tribulations it behoveth us to enter into the reign of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having appointed to them by vote elders in every assembly, having prayed with fastings, they commended them to the Lord in whom they had believed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having passed through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having spoken in Perga the word, they went down to Attalia,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thence did sail to Antioch, whence they had been given by the grace of God for the work that they fulfilled;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come and gathered together the assembly, they declared as many things as God did with them, and that He did open to the nations a door of faith;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they abode there not a little time with the disciples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain having come down from Judea, were teaching the brethren -- 'If ye be not circumcised after the custom of Moses, ye are not able to be saved;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: there having been, therefore, not a little dissension and disputation to Paul and Barnabas with them, they arranged for Paul and Barnabas, and certain others of them, to go up unto the apostles and elders to Jerusalem about this question,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they indeed, then, having been sent forward by the assembly, were passing through Phenice and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the nations, and they were causing great joy to all the brethren.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having come to Jerusalem, they were received by the assembly, and the apostles, and the elders, they declared also as many things as God did with them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there rose up certain of those of the sect of the Pharisees who believed, saying -- 'It behoveth to circumcise them, to command them also to keep the law of Moses.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were gathered together the apostles and the elders, to see about this matter,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there having been much disputing, Peter having risen up said unto them, 'Men, brethren, ye know that from former days, God among us did make choice, through my mouth, for the nations to hear the word of the good news, and to believe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the heart-knowing God did bare them testimony, having given to them the Holy Spirit, even as also to us,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and did put no difference also between us and them, by the faith having purified their hearts;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: now, therefore, why do ye tempt God, to put a yoke upon the neck of the disciples, which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but, through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, we believe to be saved, even as also they.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the multitude did keep silence, and were hearkening to Barnabas and Paul, declaring as many signs and wonders as God did among the nations through them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and after they are silent, James answered, saying, 'Men, brethren, hearken to me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Simeon did declare how at first God did look after to take out of the nations a people for His name,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to this agree the words of the prophets, as it hath been written:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: After these things I will turn back, and I will build again the tabernacle of David, that is fallen down, and its ruins I will build again, and will set it upright --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that the residue of men may seek after the Lord, and all the nations, upon whom My name hath been called, saith the Lord, who is doing all these things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Known from the ages to God are all His works;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore I judge: not to trouble those who from the nations do turn back to God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but to write to them to abstain from the pollutions of the idols, and the whoredom, and the strangled thing; and the blood;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Moses from former generations in every city hath those preaching him -- in the synagogues every sabbath being read.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders, with the whole assembly, chosen men out of themselves to send to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas -- Judas surnamed Barsabas, and Silas, leading men among the brethren --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having written through their hand thus: 'The apostles, and the elders, and the brethren, to those in Antioch, and Syria, and Cilicia, brethren, who [are] of the nations, greeting;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: seeing we have heard that certain having gone forth from us did trouble you with words, subverting your souls, saying to be circumcised and to keep the law, to whom we did give no charge,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: it seemed good to us, having come together with one accord, chosen men to send unto you, with our beloved Barnabas and Paul --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: men who have given up their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: we have sent, therefore, Judas and Silas, and they by word are telling the same things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For it seemed good to the Holy Spirit, and to us, no more burden to lay upon you, except these necessary things:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to abstain from things offered to idols, and blood, and a strangled thing, and whoredom; from which keeping yourselves, ye shall do well; be strong!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They then, indeed, having been let go, went to Antioch, and having brought the multitude together, did deliver the epistle,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having read, did rejoice for the consolation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Judas also and Silas, being themselves also prophets, through much discourse did exhort the brethren, and confirm,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having passed some time, they were let go with peace from the brethren unto the apostles;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it seemed good to Silas to remain there still.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul and Barnabas continued in Antioch, teaching and proclaiming good news -- with many others also -- the word of the Lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and after certain days, Paul said unto Barnabas, 'Having turned back again, we may look after our brethren, in every city in which we have preached the word of the Lord -- how they are.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Barnabas counselled to take with [them] John called Mark,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul was not thinking it good to take him with them who withdrew from them from Pamphylia, and did not go with them to the work;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: there came, therefore, a sharp contention, so that they were parted from one another, and Barnabas having taken Mark, did sail to Cyprus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul having chosen Silas, went forth, having been given up to the grace of God by the brethren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he went through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the assemblies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he came to Derbe and Lystra, and lo, a certain disciple was there, by name Timotheus son of a certain woman, a believing Jewess, but of a father, a Greek,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who was well testified to by the brethren in Lystra and Iconium;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this one did Paul wish to go forth with him, and having taken [him], he circumcised him, because of the Jews who are in those places, for they all knew his father -- that he was a Greek.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as they were going on through the cities, they were delivering to them the decrees to keep, that have been judged by the apostles and the elders who [are] in Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then, indeed, were the assemblies established in the faith, and were abounding in number every day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gone through Phrygia and the region of Galatia, having been forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having gone toward Mysia, they were trying to go on toward Bithynia, and the Spirit did not suffer them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having passed by Mysia, they came down to Troas.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a vision through the night appeared to Paul -- a certain man of Macedonia was standing, calling upon him, and saying, 'Having passed through to Macedonia, help us;' --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when he saw the vision, immediately we endeavoured to go forth to Macedonia, assuredly gathering that the Lord hath called us to preach good news to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having set sail, therefore, from Troas, we came with a straight course to Samothracia, on the morrow also to Neapolis,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thence also to Philippi, which is a principal city of the part of Macedonia -- a colony. And we were in this city abiding certain days,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: on the sabbath-day also we went forth outside of the city, by a river, where there used to be prayer, and having sat down, we were speaking to the women who came together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a certain woman, by name Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, worshipping God, was hearing, whose heart the Lord did open to attend to the things spoken by Paul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when she was baptized, and her household, she did call upon us, saying, 'If ye have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, having entered into my house, remain;' and she constrained us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass in our going on to prayer, a certain maid, having a spirit of Python, did meet us, who brought much employment to her masters by soothsaying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: she having followed Paul and us, was crying, saying, 'These men are servants of the Most High God, who declare to us a way of salvation;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this she was doing for many days, but Paul having been grieved, and having turned, said to the spirit, 'I command thee, in the name of Jesus Christ, to come forth from her;' and it came forth the same hour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And her masters having seen that the hope of their employment was gone, having caught Paul and Silas, drew [them] to the market-place, unto the rulers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having brought them to the magistrates, they said, 'These men do exceedingly trouble our city, being Jews;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they proclaim customs that are not lawful for us to receive nor to do, being Romans.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the multitude rose up together against them, and the magistrates having torn their garments from them, were commanding to beat [them] with rods,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: many blows also having laid upon them, they cast them to prison, having given charge to the jailor to keep them safely,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who such a charge having received, did put them to the inner prison, and their feet made fast in the stocks.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And at midnight Paul and Silas praying, were singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were hearing them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and suddenly a great earthquake came, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken, opened also presently were all the doors, and of all -- the bands were loosed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the jailor having come out of sleep, and having seen the doors of the prison open, having drawn a sword, was about to kill himself, supposing the prisoners to be fled,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul cried out with a loud voice, saying, 'Thou mayest not do thyself any harm, for we are all here.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, having asked for a light, he sprang in, and trembling he fell down before Paul and Silas,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having brought them forth, said, 'Sirs, what must I do -- that I may be saved?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said, 'Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved -- thou and thy house;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they spake to him the word of the Lord, and to all those in his household;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken them, in that hour of the night, he did bathe [them] from the blows, and was baptized, himself and all his presently,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having brought them also into his house, he set food before [them], and was glad with all the household, he having believed in God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And day having come, the magistrates sent the rod-bearers, saying, 'Let those men go;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the jailor told these words unto Paul -- 'The magistrates have sent, that ye may be let go; now, therefore, having gone forth go on in peace;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul said to them, 'Having beaten us publicly uncondemned -- men, Romans being -- they did cast [us] to prison, and now privately do they cast us forth! why no! but having come themselves, let them bring us forth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the rod-bearers told to the magistrates these sayings, and they were afraid, having heard that they are Romans,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come, they besought them, and having brought [them] forth, they were asking [them] to go forth from the city;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, having gone forth out of the prison, entered into [the house of] Lydia, and having seen the brethren, they comforted them, and went forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having passed through Amphipolis, and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was the synagogue of the Jews,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and according to the custom of Paul, he went in unto them, and for three sabbaths he was reasoning with them from the Writings,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: opening and alleging, 'That the Christ it behoved to suffer, and to rise again out of the dead, and that this is the Christ -- Jesus whom I proclaim to you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain of them did believe, and attached themselves to Paul and to Silas, also of the worshipping Greeks a great multitude, of the principal women also not a few.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the unbelieving Jews, having been moved with envy, and having taken to them of the loungers certain evil men, and having made a crowd, were setting the city in an uproar; having assailed also the house of Jason, they were seeking them to bring [them] to the populace,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not having found them, they drew Jason and certain brethren unto the city rulers, calling aloud -- 'These, having put the world in commotion, are also here present,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom Jason hath received; and these all do contrary to the decrees of Caesar, saying another to be king -- Jesus.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they troubled the multitude and the city rulers, hearing these things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taking security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the brethren immediately, through the night, sent forth both Paul and Silas to Berea, who having come, went to the synagogue of the Jews;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these were more noble than those in Thessalonica, they received the word with all readiness of mind, every day examining the Writings whether those things were so;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: many, indeed, therefore, of them did believe, and of the honourable Greek women and men not a few.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when the Jews from Thessalonica knew that also in Berea was the word of God declared by Paul, they came thither also, agitating the multitudes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and then immediately the brethren sent forth Paul, to go on as it were to the sea, but both Silas and Timothy were remaining there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those conducting Paul, brought him unto Athens, and having received a command unto Silas and Timotheus that with all speed they may come unto him, they departed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul waiting for them in Athens, his spirit was stirred in him, beholding the city wholly given to idolatry,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore, indeed, he was reasoning in the synagogue with the Jews, and with the worshipping persons, and in the market-place every day with those who met with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain of the Epicurean and of the Stoic philosophers, were meeting together to see him, and some were saying, 'What would this seed picker wish to say?' and others, 'Of strange demons he doth seem to be an announcer;' because Jesus and the rising again he did proclaim to them as good news,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having also taken him, unto the Areopagus they brought [him], saying, 'Are we able to know what [is] this new teaching that is spoken by thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for certain strange things thou dost bring to our ears? we wish, then, to know what these things would wish to be;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all Athenians, and the strangers sojourning, for nothing else were at leisure but to say something, and to hear some newer thing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul, having stood in the midst of the Areopagus, said, 'Men, Athenians, in all things I perceive you as over-religious;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for passing through and contemplating your objects of worship, I found also an erection on which had been inscribed: To God -- unknown; whom, therefore -- not knowing -- ye do worship, this One I announce to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'God, who did make the world, and all things in it, this One, of heaven and of earth being Lord, in temples made with hands doth not dwell,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: neither by the hands of men is He served -- needing anything, He giving to all life, and breath, and all things;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He made also of one blood every nation of men, to dwell upon all the face of the earth -- having ordained times before appointed, and the bounds of their dwellings --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to seek the Lord, if perhaps they did feel after Him and find, -- though, indeed, He is not far from each one of us,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for in Him we live, and move, and are; as also certain of your poets have said: For of Him also we are offspring.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Being, therefore, offspring of God, we ought not to think the Godhead to be like to gold, or silver, or stone, graving of art and device of man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the times, indeed, therefore, of the ignorance God having overlooked, doth now command all men everywhere to reform,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because He did set a day in which He is about to judge the world in righteousness, by a man whom He did ordain, having given assurance to all, having raised him out of the dead.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having heard of a rising again of the dead, some, indeed, were mocking, but others said, 'We will hear thee again concerning this;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and so Paul went forth from the midst of them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and certain men having cleaved to him, did believe, among whom [is] also Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman, by name Damaris, and others with them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after these things, Paul having departed out of Athens, came to Corinth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having found a certain Jew, by name Aquilas, of Pontus by birth, lately come from Italy, and Priscilla his wife -- because of Claudius having directed all the Jews to depart out of Rome -- he came to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and because of being of the same craft, he did remain with them, and was working, for they were tent-makers as to craft;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was reasoning in the synagogue every sabbath, persuading both Jews and Greeks.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when both Silas and Timotheus came down from Macedonia, Paul was pressed in the Spirit, testifying fully to the Jews Jesus the Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on their resisting and speaking evil, having shaken [his] garments, he said unto them, 'Your blood [is] upon your head -- I am clean; henceforth to the nations I will go on.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having departed thence, he went to the house of a certain one, by name Justus, a worshipper of God, whose house was adjoining the synagogue,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue did believe in the Lord with all his house, and many of the Corinthians hearing were believing, and they were being baptized.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord said through a vision in the night to Paul, 'Be not afraid, but be speaking and thou mayest be not silent;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because I am with thee, and no one shall set on thee to do thee evil; because I have much people in this city;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he continued a year and six months, teaching among them the word of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Gallio being proconsul of Achaia, the Jews made a rush with one accord upon Paul, and brought him unto the tribunal,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying -- 'Against the law this one doth persuade men to worship God;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul being about to open [his] mouth, Gallio said unto the Jews, 'If, indeed, then, it was anything unrighteous, or an act of wicked profligacy, O Jews, according to reason I had borne with you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but if it is a question concerning words and names, and of your law, look ye yourselves [to it], for a judge of these things I do not wish to be,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he drave them from the tribunal;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the Greeks having taken Sosthenes, the chief man of the synagogue, were beating [him] before the tribunal, and not even for these things was Gallio caring.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul having remained yet a good many days, having taken leave of the brethren, was sailing to Syria -- and with him [are] Priscilla and Aquilas -- having shorn [his] head in Cenchera, for he had a vow;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he came down to Ephesus, and did leave them there, and he himself having entered into the synagogue did reason with the Jews:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having requested [him] to remain a longer time with them, he did not consent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but took leave of them, saying, 'It behoveth me by all means the coming feast to keep at Jerusalem, and again I will return unto you -- God willing.' And he sailed from Ephesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come down to Caesarea, having gone up, and having saluted the assembly, he went down to Antioch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having made some stay he went forth, going through in order the region of Galatia and Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a certain Jew, Apollos by name, an Alexandrian by birth, a man of eloquence, being mighty in the Writings, came to Ephesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this one was instructed in the way of the Lord, and being fervent in the Spirit, was speaking and teaching exactly the things about the Lord, knowing only the baptism of John;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this one also began to speak boldly in the synagogue, and Aquilas and Priscilla having heard of him, took him to [them], and did more exactly expound to him the way of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he being minded to go through into Achaia, the brethren wrote to the disciples, having exhorted them to receive him, who having come, did help them much who have believed through the grace,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for powerfully the Jews he was refuting publicly, shewing through the Writings Jesus to be the Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in Apollos' being in Corinth, Paul having gone through the upper parts, came to Ephesus, and having found certain disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he said unto them, 'The Holy Spirit did ye receive -- having believed?' and they said unto him, 'But we did not even hear whether there is any Holy Spirit;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto them, 'To what, then, were ye baptized?' and they said, 'To John's baptism.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul said, 'John, indeed, did baptize with a baptism of reformation, saying to the people that in him who is coming after him they should believe -- that is, in the Christ -- Jesus;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, having heard, were baptized -- to the name of the Lord Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul having laid on them [his] hands, the Holy Spirit came upon them, they were speaking also with tongues, and prophesying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the men were, as it were, twelve.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gone into the synagogue, he was speaking boldly for three months, reasoning and persuading the things concerning the reign of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when certain were hardened and were disbelieving, speaking evil of the way before the multitude, having departed from them, he did separate the disciples, every day reasoning in the school of a certain Tyrannus.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this happened for two years so that all those dwelling in Asia did hear the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: mighty works also -- not common -- was God working through the hands of Paul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that even unto the ailing were brought from his body handkerchiefs or aprons, and the sicknesses departed from them; the evil spirits also went forth from them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain of the wandering exorcist Jews, took upon [them] to name over those having the evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, 'We adjure you by Jesus, whom Paul doth preach;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there were certain -- seven sons of Sceva, a Jew, a chief priest -- who are doing this thing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the evil spirit, answering, said, 'Jesus I know, and Paul I am acquainted with; and ye -- who are ye?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man, in whom was the evil spirit, leaping upon them, and having overcome them, prevailed against them, so that naked and wounded they did flee out of that house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this became known to all, both Jews and Greeks, who are dwelling at Ephesus, and fear fell upon them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was being magnified,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: many also of those who did believe were coming, confessing and declaring their acts,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and many of those who had practised the curious arts, having brought the books together, were burning [them] before all; and they reckoned together the prices of them, and found [it] five myriads of silverlings;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so powerfully was the word of God increasing and prevailing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when these things were fulfilled, Paul purposed in the Spirit, having gone through Macedonia and Achaia, to go on to Jerusalem, saying -- 'After my being there, it behoveth me also to see Rome;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having sent to Macedonia two of those ministering to him -- Timotheus and Erastus -- he himself stayed a time in Asia.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there came, at that time, not a little stir about the way,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for a certain one, Demetrius by name, a worker in silver, making silver sanctuaries of Artemis, was bringing to the artificers gain not a little,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom, having brought in a crowd together, and those who did work about such things, he said, 'Men, ye know that by this work we have our wealth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye see and hear, that not only at Ephesus, but almost in all Asia, this Paul, having persuaded, did turn away a great multitude, saying, that they are not gods who are made by hands;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not only is this department in danger for us of coming into disregard, but also, that of the great goddess Artemis the temple is to be reckoned for nothing, and also her greatness is about to be brought down, whom all Asia and the world doth worship.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they having heard, and having become full of wrath, were crying out, saying, 'Great [is] the Artemis of the Ephesians!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the whole city was filled with confusion, they rushed also with one accord into the theatre, having caught Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians, Paul's fellow-travellers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on Paul's purposing to enter in unto the populace, the disciples were not suffering him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and certain also of the chief men of Asia, being his friends, having sent unto him, were entreating him not to venture himself into the theatre.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Some indeed, therefore, were calling out one thing, and some another, for the assembly was confused, and the greater part did not know for what they were come together;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and out of the multitude they put forward Alexander -- the Jews thrusting him forward -- and Alexander having beckoned with the hand, wished to make defence to the populace,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having known that he is a Jew, one voice came out of all, for about two hours, crying, 'Great [is] the Artemis of the Ephesians!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the public clerk having quieted the multitude, saith, 'Men, Ephesians, why, who is the man that doth not know that the city of the Ephesians is a devotee of the great goddess Artemis, and of that which fell down from Zeus?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: these things, then, not being to be gainsaid, it is necessary for you to be quiet, and to do nothing rashly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For ye brought these men, who are neither temple-robbers nor speaking evil of your goddess;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if indeed, therefore, Demetrius and the artificers with him with any one have a matter, court [days] are held, and there are proconsuls; let them accuse one another.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if ye seek after anything concerning other matters, in the legal assembly it shall be determined;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for we are also in peril of being accused of insurrection in regard to this day, there being no occasion by which we shall be able to give an account of this concourse;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these things having said, he dismissed the assembly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after the ceasing of the tumult, Paul having called near the disciples, and having embraced [them], went forth to go on to Macedonia;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gone through those parts, and having exhorted them with many words, he came to Greece;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having made also three months' [stay] -- a counsel of the Jews having been against him -- being about to set forth to Syria, there came [to him] a resolution of returning through Macedonia.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were accompanying him unto Asia, Sopater of Berea, and of Thessalonians Aristarchus and Secundus, and Gaius of Derbe, and Timotheus, and of Asiatics Tychicus and Trophimus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: these, having gone before, did remain for us in Troas,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we sailed, after the days of the unleavened food, from Philippi, and came unto them to Troas in five days, where we abode seven days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on the first of the week, the disciples having been gathered together to break bread, Paul was discoursing to them, about to depart on the morrow, he was also continuing the discourse till midnight,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there were many lamps in the upper chamber where they were gathered together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there was sitting a certain youth, by name Eutychus, upon the window -- being borne down by a deep sleep, Paul discoursing long -- he having sunk down from the sleep, fell down from the third story, and was lifted up dead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul, having gone down, fell upon him, and having embraced [him], said, 'Make no tumult, for his life is in him;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come up, and having broken bread, and having tasted, for a long time also having talked -- till daylight, so he went forth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they brought up the lad alive, and were comforted in no ordinary measure.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we having gone before unto the ship, did sail to Assos, thence intending to take in Paul, for so he had arranged, intending himself to go on foot;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when he met with us at Assos, having taken him up, we came to Mitylene,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thence having sailed, on the morrow we came over-against Chios, and the next day we arrived at Samos, and having remained in Trogyllium, on the following day we came to Miletus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Paul decided to sail past Ephesus, that there may not be to him a loss of time in Asia, for he hasted, if it were possible for him, on the day of the Pentecost to be at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And from Miletus, having sent to Ephesus, he called for the elders of the assembly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when they were come unto him, he said to them, 'Ye -- ye know from the first day in which I came to Asia, how, with you at all times I was;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: serving the Lord with all humility, and many tears, and temptations, that befell me in the counsels of the Jews against [me];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how nothing I did keep back of what things are profitable, not to declare to you, and to teach you publicly, and in every house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: testifying fully both to Jews and Greeks, toward God reformation, and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, lo, I -- bound in the Spirit -- go on to Jerusalem, the things that shall befall me in it not knowing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: save that the Holy Spirit in every city doth testify fully, saying, that for me bonds and tribulations remain;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I make account of none of these, neither do I count my life precious to myself, so that I finish my course with joy, and the ministration that I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify fully the good news of the grace of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And now, lo, I have known that no more shall ye see my face, -- ye all among whom I did go preaching the reign of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore I take you to witness this day, that I [am] clear from the blood of all,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I did not keep back from declaring to you all the counsel of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed, therefore, to yourselves, and to all the flock, among which the Holy Spirit made you overseers, to feed the assembly of God that He acquired through His own blood,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I have known this, that there shall enter in, after my departing, grievous wolves unto you, not sparing the flock,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of your own selves there shall arise men, speaking perverse things, to draw away the disciples after them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Therefore, watch, remembering that three years, night and day, I did not cease with tears warning each one;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, I commend you, brethren, to God, and to the word of His grace, that is able to build up, and to give you an inheritance among all those sanctified.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The silver or gold or garments of no one did I covet;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye yourselves know that to my necessities, and to those who were with me, minister did these hands;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all things I did shew you, that, thus labouring, it behoveth [us] to partake with the ailing, to be mindful also of the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these things having said, having bowed his knees, with them all, he did pray,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there came a great weeping to all, and having fallen upon the neck of Paul, they were kissing him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: sorrowing most of all for the word that he had said -- that they are about no more to see his face; and they were accompanying him to the ship.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, at our sailing, having been parted from them, having run direct, we came to Coos, and the succeeding [day] to Rhodes, and thence to Patara,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having found a ship passing over to Phenicia, having gone on board, we sailed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having discovered Cyprus, and having left it on the left, we were sailing to Syria, and did land at Tyre, for there was the ship discharging the lading.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having found out the disciples, we tarried there seven days, and they said to Paul, through the Spirit, not to go up to Jerusalem;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but when it came that we completed the days, having gone forth, we went on, all bringing us on the way, with women and children, unto the outside of the city, and having bowed the knees upon the shore, we prayed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having embraced one another, we embarked in the ship, and they returned to their own friends.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we, having finished the course, from Tyre came down to Ptolemais, and having saluted the brethren, we remained one day with them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the morrow Paul and his company having gone forth, we came to Caesarea, and having entered into the house of Philip the evangelist -- who is of the seven -- we remained with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this one had four daughters, virgins, prophesying.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we remaining many more days, there came down a certain one from Judea, a prophet, by name Agabus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having come unto us, and having taken up the girdle of Paul, having bound also his own hands and feet, said, 'Thus saith the Holy Spirit, The man whose is this girdle -- so shall the Jews in Jerusalem bind, and they shall deliver [him] up to the hands of nations.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when we heard these things, we called upon [him] -- both we, and those of that place -- not to go up to Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul answered, 'What do ye -- weeping, and crushing mine heart? for I, not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem, am ready, for the name of the Lord Jesus;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he not being persuaded, we were silent, saying, 'The will of the Lord be done.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after these days, having taken [our] vessels, we were going up to Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there went also of the disciples from Caesarea with us, bringing with them him with whom we may lodge, a certain Mnason of Cyprus, an aged disciple.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we having come to Jerusalem, the brethren did gladly receive us,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the morrow Paul was going in with us unto James, all the elders also came,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having saluted them, he was declaring, one by one, each of the things God did among the nations through his ministration,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having heard, were glorifying the Lord. They said also to him, 'Thou seest, brother, how many myriads there are of Jews who have believed, and all are zealous of the law,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they are instructed concerning thee, that apostasy from Moses thou dost teach to all Jews among the nations, saying -- Not to circumcise the children, nor after the customs to walk;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: what then is it? certainly the multitude it behoveth to come together, for they will hear that thou hast come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'This, therefore, do that we say to thee: We have four men having a vow on themselves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: these having taken, be purified with them, and be at expence with them, that they may shave the head, and all may know that the things of which they have been instructed concerning thee are nothing, but thou dost walk -- thyself also -- the law keeping.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And concerning those of the nations who have believed, we have written, having given judgment, that they observe no such thing, except to keep themselves both from idol-sacrifices, and blood, and a strangled thing, and whoredom.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Paul, having taken the men, on the following day, with them having purified himself, was entering into the temple, announcing the fulfilment of the days of the purification, till the offering was offered for each one of them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, as the seven days were about to be fully ended, the Jews from Asia having beheld him in the temple, were stirring up all the multitude, and they laid hands upon him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: crying out, 'Men, Israelites, help! this is the man who, against the people, and the law, and this place, all everywhere is teaching; and further, also, Greeks he brought into the temple, and hath defiled this holy place;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for they had seen before Trophimus, the Ephesian, in the city with him, whom they were supposing that Paul brought into the temple.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the city also was moved and there was a running together of the people, and having laid hold on Paul, they were drawing him out of the temple, and immediately were the doors shut,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they seeking to kill him, a rumour came to the chief captain of the band that all Jerusalem hath been thrown into confusion,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who, at once, having taken soldiers and centurions, ran down upon them, and they having seen the chief captain and the soldiers, did leave off beating Paul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then the chief captain, having come nigh, took him, and commanded [him] to be bound with two chains, and was inquiring who he may be, and what it is he hath been doing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and some were crying out one thing, and some another, among the multitude, and not being able to know the certainty because of the tumult, he commanded him to be carried to the castle,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when he came upon the steps, it happened he was borne by the soldiers, because of the violence of the multitude,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the crowd of the people was following after, crying, 'Away with him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul being about to be led into the castle, saith to the chief captain, 'Is it permitted to me to say anything unto thee?' and he said, 'Greek dost thou know?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: art not thou, then, the Egyptian who before these days made an uprising, and did lead into the desert the four thousand men of the assassins?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul said, 'I, indeed, am a man, a Jew, of Tarsus of Cilicia, of no mean city a citizen; and I beseech thee, suffer me to speak unto the people.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he having given him leave, Paul having stood upon the stairs, did beckon with the hand to the people, and there having been a great silence, he spake unto them in the Hebrew dialect, saying:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Men, brethren, and fathers, hear my defence now unto you;' --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having heard that in the Hebrew dialect he was speaking to them, gave the more silence, and he saith, --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I, indeed, am a man, a Jew, having been born in Tarsus of Cilicia, and brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, having been taught according to the exactitude of a law of the fathers, being zealous of God, as all ye are to-day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this way I persecuted unto death, binding and delivering up to prisons both men and women,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as also the chief priest doth testify to me, and all the eldership; from whom also having received letters unto the brethren, to Damascus, I was going on, to bring also those there bound to Jerusalem that they might be punished,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it came to pass, in my going on and coming nigh to Damascus, about noon, suddenly out of the heaven there shone a great light round about me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I fell also to the ground, and I heard a voice saying to me, Saul, Saul, why me dost thou persecute?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? and he said unto me, I am Jesus the Nazarene whom thou dost persecute --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they who are with me the light did see, and became afraid, and the voice they heard not of him who is speaking to me --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I said, What shall I do, Lord? and the Lord said unto me, Having risen, go on to Damascus, and there it shall be told thee concerning all things that have been appointed for thee to do.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when I did not see from the glory of that light, being led by the hand by those who are with me, I came to Damascus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a certain one, Ananias, a pious man according to the law, being testified to by all the Jews dwelling [there],

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having come unto me and stood by [me], said to me, Saul, brother, look up; and I the same hour did look up to him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, The God of our fathers did choose thee beforehand to know His will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice out of his mouth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, why tarriest thou? having risen, baptize thyself, and wash away thy sins, calling upon the name of the Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it came to pass when I returned to Jerusalem, and while I was praying in the temple, I came into a trance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I saw him saying to me, Haste and go forth in haste out of Jerusalem, because they will not receive thy testimony concerning me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I said, Lord, they -- they know that I was imprisoning and was scourging in every synagogue those believing on thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when the blood of thy witness Stephen was being poured forth, I also was standing by and assenting to his death, and keeping the garments of those putting him to death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto me, Go, because to nations far off I will send thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they were hearing him unto this word, and they lifted up their voice, saying, 'Away from the earth with such an one; for it is not fit for him to live.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
