translations.text: And they crying out and casting up their garments, and throwing dust into the air,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, saying, 'By scourges let him be examined;' that he might know for what cause they were crying so against him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as he was stretching him with the thongs, Paul said unto the centurion who was standing by, 'A man, a Roman, uncondemned -- is it lawful to you to scourge;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the centurion having heard, having gone near to the chief captain, told, saying, 'Take heed what thou art about to do, for this man is a Roman;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief captain having come near, said to him, 'Tell me, art thou a Roman?' and he said, 'Yes;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief captain answered, 'I, with a great sum, did obtain this citizenship;' but Paul said, 'But I have been even born [so].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Immediately, therefore, they departed from him who are about to examine him, and the chief captain also was afraid, having learned that he is a Roman, and because he had bound him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the morrow, intending to know the certainty wherefore he is accused by the Jews, he did loose him from the bonds, and commanded the chief priests and all their sanhedrim to come, and having brought down Paul, he set [him] before them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul having earnestly beheld the sanhedrim, said, 'Men, brethren, I in all good conscience have lived to God unto this day;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief priest Ananias commanded those standing by him to smite him on the mouth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then Paul said unto him, 'God is about to smite thee, thou whitewashed wall, and thou -- thou dost sit judging me according to the law, and, violating law, dost order me to be smitten!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those who stood by said, 'The chief priest of God dost thou revile?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul said, 'I did not know, brethren, that he is chief priest: for it hath been written, Of the ruler of thy people thou shalt not speak evil;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul having known that the one part are Sadducees, and the other Pharisees, cried out in the sanhedrim, 'Men, brethren, I am a Pharisee -- son of a Pharisee -- concerning hope and rising again of dead men I am judged.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he having spoken this, there came a dissension of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees, and the crowd was divided,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Sadducees, indeed, say there is no rising again, nor messenger, nor spirit, but Pharisees confess both.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there came a great cry, and the scribes of the Pharisees' part having arisen, were striving, saying, 'No evil do we find in this man; and if a spirit spake to him, or a messenger, we may not fight against God;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a great dissension having come, the chief captain having been afraid lest Paul may be pulled to pieces by them, commanded the soldiery, having gone down, to take him by force out of the midst of them, and to bring [him] to the castle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on the following night, the Lord having stood by him, said, 'Take courage, Paul, for as thou didst fully testify the things concerning me at Jerusalem, so it behoveth thee also at Rome to testify.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And day having come, certain of the Jews having made a concourse, did anathematize themselves, saying neither to eat nor to drink till they may kill Paul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were more than forty who made this conspiracy by oath,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who having come near to the chief priests and to the elders said, 'With an anathema we did anathematize ourselves -- to taste nothing till we have killed Paul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: now, therefore, ye, signify ye to the chief captain, with the sanhedrim, that to-morrow he may bring him down unto you, as being about to know more exactly the things concerning him; and we, before his coming nigh, are ready to put him to death.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the son of Paul's sister having heard of the lying in wait, having gone and entered into the castle, told Paul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul having called near one of the centurions, said, 'This young man lead unto the chief captain, for he hath something to tell him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He indeed, then, having taken him, brought him unto the chief captain, and saith, 'The prisoner Paul, having called me near, asked [me] this young man to bring unto thee, having something to say to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief captain having taken him by the hand, and having withdrawn by themselves, inquired, 'What is that which thou hast to tell me?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said -- 'The Jews agreed to request thee, that to-morrow to the sanhedrim thou mayest bring down Paul, as being about to enquire something more exactly concerning him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou, therefore, mayest thou not yield to them, for there lie in wait for him of them more than forty men, who did anathematize themselves -- not to eat nor to drink till they kill him, and now they are ready, waiting for the promise from thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The chief captain, then, indeed, let the young man go, having charged [him] to tell no one, 'that these things thou didst shew unto me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having called near a certain two of the centurions, he said, 'Make ready soldiers two hundred, that they may go on unto Caesarea, and horsemen seventy, and spearmen two hundred, from the third hour of the night;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: beasts also provide, that, having set Paul on, they may bring him safe unto Felix the governor;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he having written a letter after this description:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Claudius Lysias, to the most noble governor Felix, hail:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This man having been taken by the Jews, and being about to be killed by them -- having come with the soldiery, I rescued him, having learned that he is a Roman;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, intending to know the cause for which they were accusing him, I brought him down to their sanhedrim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom I found accused concerning questions of their law, and having no accusation worthy of death or bonds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a plot having been intimated to me against this man -- about to be of the Jews -- at once I sent unto thee, having given command also to the accusers to say the things against him before thee; be strong.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then, indeed, the soldiers according to that directed them, having taken up Paul, brought him through the night to Antipatris,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the morrow, having suffered the horsemen to go on with him, they returned to the castle;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: those having entered into Caesarea, and delivered the letter to the governor, did present also Paul to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the governor having read [it], and inquired of what province he is, and understood that [he is] from Cilicia;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I will hear thee -- said he -- when thine accusers also may have come;' he also commanded him to be kept in the praetorium of Herod.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after five days came down the chief priest Ananias, with the elders, and a certain orator -- Tertullus, and they made manifest to the governor [the things] against Paul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having been called, Tertullus began to accuse [him], saying, 'Much peace enjoying through thee, and worthy deeds being done to this nation through thy forethought,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: always, also, and everywhere we receive it, most noble Felix, with all thankfulness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and that I may not be further tedious to thee, I pray thee to hear us concisely in thy gentleness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for having found this man a pestilence, and moving a dissension to all the Jews through the world -- a ringleader also of the sect of the Nazarenes --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who also the temple did try to profane, whom also we took, and according to our law did wish to judge,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Lysias the chief captain having come near, with much violence, out of our hands did take away,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having commanded his accusers to come to thee, from whom thou mayest be able, thyself having examined, to know concerning all these things of which we accuse him;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Jews also agreed, professing these things to be so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Paul answered -- the governor having beckoned to him to speak -- 'Knowing [that] for many years thou hast been a judge to this nation, the more cheerfully the things concerning myself I do answer;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou being able to know that it is not more than twelve days to me since I went up to worship in Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and neither in the temple did they find me reasoning with any one, or making a dissension of the multitude, nor in the synagogues, nor in the city;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor are they able to prove against me the things concerning which they now accuse me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I confess this to thee, that, according to the way that they call a sect, so serve I the God of the fathers, believing all things that in the law and the prophets have been written,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having hope toward God, which they themselves also wait for, [that] there is about to be a rising again of the dead, both of righteous and unrighteous;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in this I do exercise myself, to have a conscience void of offence toward God and men always.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And after many years I came, about to do kind acts to my nation, and offerings,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in which certain Jews from Asia did find me purified in the temple, not with multitude, nor with tumult,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom it behoveth to be present before thee, and to accuse, if they had anything against me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or let these same say if they found any unrighteousness in me in my standing before the sanhedrim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: except concerning this one voice, in which I cried, standing among them -- Concerning a rising again of the dead I am judged to-day by you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having heard these things, Felix delayed them -- having known more exactly of the things concerning the way -- saying, 'When Lysias the chief captain may come down, I will know fully the things concerning you;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having given also a direction to the centurion to keep Paul, to let [him] also have liberty, and to forbid none of his own friends to minister or to come near to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after certain days, Felix having come with Drusilla his wife, being a Jewess, he sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith toward Christ,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he reasoning concerning righteousness, and temperance, and the judgment that is about to be, Felix, having become afraid, answered, 'For the present be going, and having got time, I will call for thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and at the same time also hoping that money shall be given to him by Paul, that he may release him, therefore, also sending for him the oftener, he was conversing with him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and two years having been fulfilled, Felix received a successor, Porcius Festus; Felix also willing to lay a favour on the Jews, left Paul bound.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Festus, therefore, having come into the province, after three days went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief priest and the principal men of the Jews made manifest to him [the things] against Paul, and were calling on him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: asking favour against him, that he may send for him to Jerusalem, making an ambush to put him to death in the way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then, indeed, Festus answered that Paul is kept in Caesarea, and himself is about speedily to go on thither,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Therefore those able among you -- saith he -- having come down together, if there be anything in this man -- let them accuse him;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having tarried among them more than ten days, having gone down to Caesarea, on the morrow having sat upon the tribunal, he commanded Paul to be brought;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having come, there stood round about the Jews who have come down from Jerusalem -- many and weighty charges they are bringing against Paul, which they were not able to prove,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he making defence -- 'Neither in regard to the law of the Jews, nor in regard to the temple, nor in regard to Caesar -- did I commit any sin.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Festus willing to lay on the Jews a favour, answering Paul, said, 'Art thou willing, to Jerusalem having gone up, there concerning these things to be judged before me?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul said, 'At the tribunal of Caesar I am standing, where it behoveth me to be judged; to Jews I did no unrighteousness, as thou dost also very well know;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if indeed I am unrighteous, and anything worthy of death have done, I deprecate not to die; and if there is none of the things of which these accuse me, no one is able to make a favour of me to them; to Caesar I appeal!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then Festus, having communed with the council, answered, 'To Caesar thou hast appealed; to Caesar thou shalt go.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain days having passed, Agrippa the king, and Bernice, came down to Caesarea saluting Festus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as they were continuing there more days, Festus submitted to the king the things concerning Paul, saying, 'There is a certain man, left by Felix, a prisoner,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: about whom, in my being at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews laid information, asking a decision against him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: unto whom I answered, that it is not a custom of Romans to make a favour of any man to die, before that he who is accused may have the accusers face to face, and may receive place of defence in regard to the charge laid against [him].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'They, therefore, having come together -- I, making no delay, on the succeeding [day] having sat upon the tribunal, did command the man to be brought,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: concerning whom the accusers, having stood up, were bringing against [him] no accusation of the things I was thinking of,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but certain questions concerning their own religion they had against him, and concerning a certain Jesus who was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I, doubting in regard to the question concerning this, said, If he would wish to go on to Jerusalem, and there to be judged concerning these things --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but Paul having appealed to be kept to the hearing of Sebastus, I did command him to be kept till I might send him unto Caesar.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Agrippa said unto Festus, 'I was wishing also myself to hear the man;' and he said, 'To-morrow thou shalt hear him;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: on the morrow, therefore -- on the coming of Agrippa and Bernice with much display, and they having entered into the audience chamber, with the chief captains also, and the principal men of the city, and Festus having ordered -- Paul was brought forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Festus said, 'King Agrippa, and all men who are present with us, ye see this one, about whom all the multitude of the Jews did deal with me, both in Jerusalem and here, crying out, He ought not to live any longer;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I, having found him to have done nothing worthy of death, and he also himself having appealed to Sebastus, I decided to send him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: concerning whom I have no certain thing to write to [my] lord, wherefore I brought him forth before you, and specially before thee, king Agrippa, that the examination having been made, I may have something to write;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it doth seem to me irrational, sending a prisoner, not also to signify the charges against him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Agrippa said unto Paul, 'It is permitted to thee to speak for thyself;' then Paul having stretched forth the hand, was making a defence:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Concerning all things of which I am accused by Jews, king Agrippa, I have thought myself happy, being about to make a defence before thee to-day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: especially knowing thee to be acquainted with all things -- both customs and questions -- among Jews; wherefore, I beseech thee, patiently to hear me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The manner of my life then, indeed, from youth -- which from the beginning was among my nation, in Jerusalem -- know do all the Jews,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: knowing me before from the first, (if they may be willing to testify,) that after the most exact sect of our worship, I lived a Pharisee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now for the hope of the promise made to the fathers by God, I have stood judged,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to which our twelve tribes, intently night and day serving, do hope to come, concerning which hope I am accused, king Agrippa, by the Jews;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: why is it judged incredible with you, if God doth raise the dead?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I, indeed, therefore, thought with myself, that against the name of Jesus of Nazareth it behoved [me] many things to do,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which also I did in Jerusalem, and many of the saints I in prison did shut up, from the chief priests having received the authority; they also being put to death, I gave my vote against them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in every synagogue, often punishing them, I was constraining [them] to speak evil, being also exceedingly mad against them, I was also persecuting [them] even unto strange cities.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'In which things, also, going on to Damascus -- with authority and commission from the chief priests --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: at mid-day, I saw in the way, O king, out of heaven, above the brightness of the sun, shining round about me a light -- and those going on with me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we all having fallen to the earth, I heard a voice speaking unto me, and saying in the Hebrew dialect, Saul, Saul, why me dost thou persecute? hard for thee against pricks to kick!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I said, Who art thou, Lord? and he said, I am Jesus whom thou dost persecute;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but rise, and stand upon thy feet, for for this I appeared to thee, to appoint thee an officer and a witness both of the things thou didst see, and of the things [in which] I will appear to thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: delivering thee from the people, and the nations, to whom now I send thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to open their eyes, to turn [them] from darkness to light, and [from] the authority of the Adversary unto God, for their receiving forgiveness of sins, and a lot among those having been sanctified, by faith that [is] toward me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Whereupon, king Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but to those in Damascus first, and to those in Jerusalem, to all the region also of Judea, and to the nations, I was preaching to reform, and to turn back unto God, doing works worthy of reformation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because of these things the Jews -- having caught me in the temple -- were endeavouring to kill [me].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Having obtained, therefore, help from God, till this day, I have stood witnessing both to small and to great, saying nothing besides the things that both the prophets and Moses spake of as about to come,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that the Christ is to suffer, whether first by a rising from the dead, he is about to proclaim light to the people and to the nations.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, he thus making a defence, Festus with a loud voice said, 'Thou art mad, Paul; much learning doth turn thee mad;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'I am not mad, most noble Festus, but of truth and soberness the sayings I speak forth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the king doth know concerning these things, before whom also I speak boldly, for none of these things, I am persuaded, are hidden from him; for this thing hath not been done in a corner;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou dost believe, king Agrippa, the prophets? I have known that thou dost believe!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Agrippa said unto Paul, 'In a little thou dost persuade me to become a Christian!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul said, 'I would have wished to God, both in a little, and in much, not only thee, but also all those hearing me to-day, to become such as I also am -- except these bonds.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, he having spoken these things, the king rose up, and the governor, Bernice also, and those sitting with them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having withdrawn, they were speaking unto one another, saying -- 'This man doth nothing worthy of death or of bonds;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Agrippa said to Festus, 'This man might have been released if he had not appealed to Caesar.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when our sailing to Italy was determined, they were delivering up both Paul and certain others, prisoners, to a centurion, by name Julius, of the band of Sebastus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having embarked in a ship of Adramyttium, we, being about to sail by the coasts of Asia, did set sail, there being with us Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: on the next [day] also we touched at Sidon, and Julius, courteously treating Paul, did permit [him], having gone on unto friends, to receive [their] care.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thence, having set sail, we sailed under Cyprus, because of the winds being contrary,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having sailed over the sea over-against Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myria of Lycia,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there the centurion having found a ship of Alexandria, sailing to Italy, did put us into it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having sailed slowly many days, and with difficulty coming over-against Cnidus, the wind not suffering us, we sailed under Crete, over-against Salmone,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hardly passing it, we came to a certain place called 'Fair Havens,' nigh to which was the city [of] Lasaea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And much time being spent, and the sailing being now dangerous -- because of the fast also being already past -- Paul was admonishing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying to them, 'Men, I perceive that with hurt, and much damage, not only of the lading and of the ship, but also of our lives -- the voyage is about to be;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the centurion to the pilot and to the shipowner gave credence more than to the things spoken by Paul;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the haven being incommodious to winter in, the more part gave counsel to sail thence also, if by any means they might be able, having attained to Phenice, [there] to winter, [which is] a haven of Crete, looking to the south-west and north-west,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a south wind blowing softly, having thought they had obtained [their] purpose, having lifted anchor, they sailed close by Crete,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not long after there arose against it a tempestuous wind, that is called Euroclydon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the ship being caught, and not being able to bear up against the wind, having given [her] up, we were borne on,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having run under a certain little isle, called Clauda, we were hardly able to become masters of the boat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which having taken up, they were using helps, undergirding the ship, and fearing lest they may fall on the quicksand, having let down the mast -- so were borne on.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we, being exceedingly tempest-tossed, the succeeding [day] they were making a clearing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the third [day] with our own hands the tackling of the ship we cast out,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and neither sun nor stars appearing for more days, and not a little tempest lying upon us, thenceforth all hope was taken away of our being saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there having been long fasting, then Paul having stood in the midst of them, said, 'It behoved [you], indeed, O men -- having hearkened to me -- not to set sail from Crete, and to save this hurt and damage;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now I exhort you to be of good cheer, for there shall be no loss of life among you -- but of the ship;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for there stood by me this night a messenger of God -- whose I am, and whom I serve --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, Be not afraid Paul; before Caesar it behoveth thee to stand; and, lo, God hath granted to thee all those sailing with thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore be of good cheer, men! for I believe God, that so it shall be, even as it hath been spoken to me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on a certain island it behoveth us to be cast.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when the fourteenth night came -- we being borne up and down in the Adria -- toward the middle of the night the sailors were supposing that some country drew nigh to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having sounded they found twenty fathoms, and having gone a little farther, and again having sounded, they found fifteen fathoms,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and fearing lest on rough places we may fall, out of the stern having cast four anchors, they were wishing day to come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the sailors seeking to flee out of the ship, and having let down the boat to the sea, in pretence as [if] out of the foreship they are about to cast anchors,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers, 'If these do not remain in the ship -- ye are not able to be saved;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then the soldiers did cut off the ropes of the boat, and suffered it to fall off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And till the day was about to be, Paul was calling upon all to partake of nourishment, saying, 'Fourteen days to-day, waiting, ye continue fasting, having taken nothing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore I call upon you to take nourishment, for this is for your safety, for of not one of you shall a hair from the head fall;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having said these things, and having taken bread, he gave thanks to God before all, and having broken [it], he began to eat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all having become of good cheer, themselves also took food,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (and we were -- all the souls in the ship -- two hundred, seventy and six),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having eaten sufficient nourishment, they were lightening the ship, casting forth the wheat into the sea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when the day came, they were not discerning the land, but a certain creek were perceiving having a beach, into which they took counsel, if possible, to thrust forward the ship,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the anchors having taken up, they were committing [it] to the sea, at the same time -- having loosed the bands of the rudders, and having hoisted up the mainsail to the wind -- they were making for the shore,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having fallen into a place of two seas, they ran the ship aground, and the fore-part, indeed, having stuck fast, did remain immoveable, but the hinder-part was broken by the violence of the waves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the soldiers' counsel was that they should kill the prisoners, lest any one having swam out should escape,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the centurion, wishing to save Paul, hindered them from the counsel, and did command those able to swim, having cast themselves out first -- to get unto the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the rest, some indeed upon boards, and some upon certain things of the ship; and thus it came to pass that all came safe unto the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having been saved, then they knew that the island is called Melita,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the foreigners were shewing us no ordinary kindness, for having kindled a fire, they received us all, because of the pressing rain, and because of the cold;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but Paul having gathered together a quantity of sticks, and having laid [them] upon the fire, a viper -- out of the heat having come -- did fasten on his hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when the foreigners saw the beast hanging from his hand, they said unto one another, 'Certainly this man is a murderer, whom, having been saved out of the sea, the justice did not suffer to live;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he then, indeed, having shaken off the beast into the fire, suffered no evil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were expecting him to be about to be inflamed, or to fall down suddenly dead, and they, expecting [it] a long time, and seeing nothing uncommon happening to him, changing [their] minds, said he was a god.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the neighbourhood of that place were lands of the principal man of the island, by name Publius, who, having received us, three days did courteously lodge [us];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it came to pass, the father of Publius with feverish heats and dysentery pressed, was laid, unto whom Paul having entered, and having prayed, having laid [his] hands on him, healed him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this, therefore, being done, the others also in the island having infirmities were coming and were healed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who also with many honours did honour us, and we setting sail -- they were lading [us] with the things that were necessary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after three months, we set sail in a ship (that had wintered in the isle) of Alexandria, with the sign Dioscuri,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having landed at Syracuse, we remained three days,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thence having gone round, we came to Rhegium, and after one day, a south wind having sprung up, the second [day] we came to Puteoli;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: where, having found brethren, we were called upon to remain with them seven days, and thus to Rome we came;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thence, the brethren having heard the things concerning us, came forth to meet us, unto Appii Forum, and Three Taverns -- whom Paul having seen, having given thanks to God, took courage.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when we came to Rome, the centurion delivered up the prisoners to the captain of the barrack, but Paul was suffered to remain by himself, with the soldier guarding him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass after three days, Paul called together those who are the principal men of the Jews, and they having come together, he said unto them: 'Men, brethren, I -- having done nothing contrary to the people, or to the customs of the fathers -- a prisoner from Jerusalem, was delivered up to the hands of the Romans;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who, having examined me, were wishing to release [me], because of their being no cause of death in me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Jews having spoken against [it], I was constrained to appeal unto Caesar -- not as having anything to accuse my nation of;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for this cause, therefore, I called for you to see and to speak with [you], for because of the hope of Israel with this chain I am bound.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they said unto him, 'We did neither receive letters concerning thee from Judea, nor did any one who came of the brethren declare or speak any evil concerning thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we think it good from thee to hear what thou dost think, for, indeed, concerning this sect it is known to us that everywhere it is spoken against;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having appointed him a day, they came, more of them unto him, to the lodging, to whom he was expounding, testifying fully the reign of God, persuading them also of the things concerning Jesus, both from the law of Moses, and the prophets, from morning till evening,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, some, indeed, were believing the things spoken, and some were not believing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And not being agreed with one another, they were going away, Paul having spoken one word -- 'Well did the Holy Spirit speak through Isaiah the prophet unto our fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, Go on unto this people and say, With hearing ye shall hear, and ye shall not understand, and seeing ye shall see, and ye shall not perceive,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for made gross was the heart of this people, and with the ears they heard heavily, and their eyes they did close, lest they may see with the eyes, and with the heart may understand, and be turned back, and I may heal them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Be it known, therefore, to you, that to the nations was sent the salvation of God, these also will hear it;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having said these things, the Jews went away, having much disputation among themselves;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Paul remained an entire two years in his own hired [house], and was receiving all those coming in unto him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: preaching the reign of God, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness -- unforbidden.
